resources.string: Manage folders, refresh library, parsing options
Audio behavior, crossfade, and background play
AI providers, API keys, and model settings
Export and recover your personal app data
Audio specs, codecs, and decoder info
Manage Telegram, Google Drive, NetEase, and more services
Folders here will be skipped when scanning your library.
Scan entire library for new and modified files.
This will completely rebuild your music library from scratch. All imported lyrics, favorites, and custom metadata will be lost. This action cannot be undone.
Automatically scan and assign .lrc files in the same folder during library sync.
Choose which source to try first when fetching lyrics.
Search online in the background when a playing song has no lyrics yet.
Fetch lyrics for every song without them (Wi-Fi only, runs in background).
Remove all imported lyrics from the database.
Everything is allowed by default. Tap a folder to mark it as excluded from scans.
Artist settings have changed. Rescan your library to apply.
Detect feat., ft., with in song titles
Show collaboration albums under main artist

PixelPlayer splits artist tags using character delimiters (/, ;, &) and word delimiters (feat., ft., vs., x). Word delimiters are matched case-insensitively. \"Extract Artists from Title\" detects patterns like (feat. Artist) in song titles. Backslash (\) can be used to escape character delimiters.
Tap a delimiter to remove it. At least one delimiter is required.
This will clear all your custom delimiters and restore the defaults. This action cannot be undone.
At least one delimiter is required
Delimiter already exists or is invalid
These keywords split artist names when surrounded by spaces. Matched case-insensitively. Tap to remove.

Word delimiters are matched case-insensitively with spaces around them.

Single-character delimiters (like "x") require spaces on both sides to avoid false matches.

Examples:
 "Drake feat. Rihanna" -> Drake, Rihanna
 "Marshmello x Bastille" -> Marshmello, Bastille
 "A vs. B" -> A, B
This will clear all your custom word delimiters and restore the default keywords. This action cannot be undone.
Choose the language used across the app interface.
Switch between light, dark, or follow system appearance.
Use complex shaped corners effectively improving aesthetics but may affect performance on low-end devices
Turn off blur effects across the app to save battery and resources.
Display lateral scrollbar on music lists for quick scrolling
Choose the appearance for the floating player.
Show codec, bitrate, and sample rate in the player progress section.
Current: %1$s. Open live preview and choose style.
Choose the appearance for the album carousel.
Choose the shape arrangement for the Your Mix collage.
Cycle through collage patterns each time you visit Home.
Choose the appearance for the navigation bar.
Show only icons and reduce the navbar height.
Adjust the corner radius of the navigation bar.
Choose how to move between Library tabs.
Choose the album colors for the player UI.
0 keeps the current tuning. Higher values stay closer to the album art\'s dominant hue.
Match the navbar shape\'s corners with your device\'s physical corners for a seamless look.
If off, removing the app from recents will stop playback.
Disable battery optimization to prevent playback interruptions.
Normalize volume levels using ReplayGain metadata from audio files.
Track: normalize each song. Album: normalize per album.
Start playing immediately after switching cast connections.
If playback paused because headphones were removed, resume automatically when they connect again.
Float 32-bit audio output. Disable if playback stutters on your device.
Not supported on this device (PCM_FLOAT AudioTrack unavailable).
Remember shuffle setting even after closing the app.
Keep the music going — automatically queue similar songs when the queue runs low.
Show previously played songs in the queue.
In Folders tab, system back navigates folder stack before leaving Library.
Tap the blurred background to close the player sheet.
Enable vibration feedback across the app.
ON — Fast & cheap. Sends minimal data (~1K tokens) to AI.
OFF — Deep context. Sends full listening profile (~8K tokens) for richer results.

You are \'Vibe-Engine\', a world-class music curator and master of sonic flow. Your goal is to construct seamless, high-fidelity listening experiences. Prioritize harmonic compatibility, logical BPM transitions, and a sophisticated balance between familiar favorites and tasteful, logic-driven discovery.

You are an avant-garde music explorer specializing in \'unexpected cohesion\'. Your mission is to break conventional genre boundaries by identifying non-obvious sonic parallels. Prioritize rare deep cuts, experimental textures, and artistic novelty while maintaining a surprising but undeniable transition logic.

You are a surgical music database architect. Your logic is driven by absolute metadata precision and rigid categorical adherence. Minimize algorithmic discovery in favor of strict genre consistency, energy-level matching, and maximizing the retrieval of highly specific user-defined preferences.

You are a master of ambient textures and low-energy flow. Focus exclusively on tracks that facilitate a \'deep focus\' or \'tranquility\' state. Prioritize acoustic warmth, minimalist arrangements, and gentle transitions, while strictly avoiding high-transient sounds or sudden shifts in dynamic range.

You are an audiophile analyst focused on production complexity and instrumentation. Prioritize tracks characterized by high dynamic range, intricate polyrhythms, and superior soundstage quality. Favor active-listening pieces that reward the listener for paying attention to technical fidelity and arrangement detail.

You are a high-momentum rhythm generator. Your philosophy centers on driving basslines, percussive intensity, and infectious grooves. Prioritize high-BPM club compatibility, syncopated energy, and continuous rhythmic tension to keep the listener\'s heart rate and motivation peak-level.
Choose sections, export a .pxpl file, and import it later. Restore only replaces the sections you select.
Browse or pick from recent backups. Selected data will replace current data.
Choose exactly what you want to include in the backup package.
Select a .pxpl backup file to inspect. You\'ll choose which sections to restore in the next step.
Previously imported backups will appear here.
Restore completed with some unresolved issues.
Restore completed with unresolved issues. Failed: %1$s
Player UI loading experiments and toggles.
Launch the onboarding setup screen for testing.
Re-creates the daily mix playlist immediately.
This will discard the current mix and generate a new one based on recent listening habits.
Clears cache and recalculates playback statistics.
This will clear the statistics cache and force a recalculation from the database history.
Rebuild all cached palette variants for every album art, or pick a single one to refresh.
This will clear cached theme data and rebuild all palette styles for %1$d unique album arts.
Rebuilding cached palette variants for %1$d unique album arts. This can take a while on large libraries.
Select a song to clear cached theme data and regenerate all palette styles from the album art.
Search by title, artist, or album
No songs with album art were found.
Simulate a crash to test the crash reporting system.
Test crash triggered from Developer Options - This is intentional for testing the crash reporting system
Uses spring animations and visual effects for lyrics. May cause frame drops on low-end devices.
Applies a depth-of-field blur to inactive lyrics.
Adjust the intensity of the blur effect.
Hold the full player content until the sheet background is fully expanded.
Delay album art and carousel until the sheet is expanded.
Delay title, artist, and lyrics/queue actions.
Delay the timeline and time labels until expansion completes.
Delay play/pause, seek, and favorite controls.
All delayed components are active. Disable \"Delay everything\" to customize each part.
Keep layout stable by rendering lightweight placeholders while components wait for expansion.
Step 3 · Choose when placeholders switch to real content
Select one mode. Threshold mode uses sliders; Drag release mode waits until you release the sheet gesture.
Enable at least one delayed component to unlock trigger mode.
How expanded the sheet must be before delayed components become visible.
Use close threshold to switch back to placeholders while collapsing.
How much collapse is required before placeholders take over again.
Drag release mode bypasses thresholds and close behavior. The swap happens only when the sheet drag gesture ends.
Placeholders keep their layout space but become invisible.
No compressed format reported hardware offload support.
No output routes were reported by Android.
Your indexed tracks match the decoders Android reports on this device.
The library reaches %1$d Hz, above the current output sample rate.
A full library rescan can fill missing MIME, bitrate, and sample-rate data.
Generate a shareable diagnostic report to help us classify playback or scan lag. It contains only device, library, and timing data — no file paths, titles, or artists.
Off by default. Records a short lag timeline for beta troubleshooting.
Manage linked providers and keep each integration under your control.
Connect a provider to manage it from this screen.
Unable to open this screen right now.
Open source music player built with its community.
No contributors found right now. Please try again later.
Recognition for collaborators with major impact.
Attribution for dependencies and earlier contributions.
The bundled third-party notice could not be loaded.
Automatically pause playback when the volume is set to 0
